channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🏴 Sweden selects French FDI frigates from Naval Group | Breaking Defense
item.description: “We have seen that Russia has increased its production of ballistic missiles over the past year. Our capability in this area (air defence) will triple with this acquisition,” Defense Minister Pål Jonson said at the press conference aboard the navy corvette today.

item.title: 🕵️ Sweden picks France’s FDI frigates in potential $4.2 billion deal | Defense News
item.description: Sweden picked the French vessel due to a combination of quick delivery time, an active production line and proven air-defense capabilities, officials said.

item.title: 🏛️ Army probes new Apache transmission problem as service rushes to ditch older helicopters | Defense One
item.description: Pilots fear the transmission woes and a new reduction in flight hours is a deadly combination.

item.title: 🌍 Germany to deploy Patriot battery to Turkey, relieving US forces on NATO’s southeastern flank | Defense News
item.description: Germany’s contribution to intercepting Iranian missiles is framed in Berlin as an exercise in burden-sharing.

item.title: 🏴 Polish officials vent worries over scrapped US troop deployment | Defense News
item.description: “More than fifty billion dollars is the sum of the purchases that we are implementing in the United States,” Poland's defense minister noted.

item.title: 🌍 The World Keeps Asking Iran the Wrong Question | Foreign Policy
item.description: Even before the Islamic Republic, the country has always wanted the same thing.

item.title: 🌍 When Refusal Doesn’t Matter: Operation Epic Fury and the Erosion of Host Nation Consent | War on the Rocks
item.description: When the U.S. military launched its war against Iran in Feb. 2026, it did not just dismantle Iranian military capabilities. It shattered the illusion that the United States would consult with its closest allies and that an ally’s refusal to grant base access can stop an American…

item.title: 🌍 The Recognition Question: Somaliland, Israeli Security Geometry, and the Red Sea Power Struggle | War on the Rocks
item.description: Since its proclamation of independence from Somalia in May 1991, Somaliland has sought international recognition for the territories under its control. Despite functioning as a de facto independent state for years, it lacked any formal recognition on the international stage…

item.title: 🏛️ Valkyrie redux: supercruising bombers could viably replace B-52s | ASPI Strategist
item.description: The US Air Force just revealed that it was launching a study of a new heavy bomber that might replace the Boeing B-52. The analysis of alternatives in the FY27 budget will look at ‘future ...

item.title: 📍 Indonesian Rupiah Falls to New Low as Global Oil Prices Surge | The Diplomat
item.description: The slump was accompanied by a 4 percent drop in Jakarta's main stock index, which is under close scrutiny from the global index provider MSCI.

item.title: 🌍 The Myth of Zero Enrichment | Foreign Policy
item.description: Tehran regards Washington’s demand as tantamount to unconditional surrender, but there may yet be a way forward.

item.title: 🏴 Orban’s Fall and Europe’s Rise | Foreign Affairs
item.description: The dawn of a strange new European consensus.

item.title: 📍 The Not-So-Quiet American | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Pope Leo takes the Vatican where Francis could not.

item.title: 📍 How Malaysia’s Semiconductor Industry Can Thrive in an Era of Strategic Competition | The Diplomat
item.description: The country occupies a uniquely strategic position due to its proven ability to balance competing geopolitical and economic forces.

item.title: 📍 Philippine Senate Kicks Off Impeachment Trial of Vice President Sara Duterte | The Diplomat
item.description: Whatever the trial's outcome, the political war between Duterte and President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. is unlikely to come to a conclusive end.

item.title: 📍 With an ‘Obstacle’ Gone, Will the Indian Government Finally Seal the Teesta Deal With Bangladesh? | The Diplomat
item.description: An agreement to share the waters of the Teesta River will not go down well in northern West Bengal.

item.title: 🌏 China’s influence in Solomons will probably survive leadership change | ASPI Strategist
item.description: China’s influence in Solomon Islands is likely to weather the accession to power on 15 May of a prime minister who’s less favourable to it than his predecessor. While Australia has improved its relationship with ...

item.title: 📍 ISIS leader killed in Africa as US commander raises force reduction concerns | Defense News
item.description: Despite recent operations, U.S. force reduction moves have ignited concerns over America's ability to stifle terror plots emanating from the continent.

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine declares its first homegrown guided aerial bomb combat-ready | Defense News
item.description: A 250-kilogram answer to Russia's daily glide-bomb campaign and Kyiv's dependence on Western precision strike capabilities for mid-range targets.

item.title: 🌍 Trump Claims He Called Off Imminent Iran Bombing Campaign At Behest Of Allies (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: The president says the strikes were set for tomorrow, but it's one of many times Trump made grand threats then supposedly pulled back at the last moment. The post Trump Claims He Called Off Imminent Iran Bombing Campaign At Behest Of Allies (Updated) appeared first on The War…

item.title: 🔐 Advanced AI models bring government to ‘reflection point,’ CIA official says | Defense One
item.description: New technologies may bring risk and opportunity for the federal government, cyber experts explained.

item.title: 🌏 What Happened When Trump Met Xi | Foreign Policy
item.description: Historian Rana Mitter on the new balance of power between Washington and Beijing.

item.title: 🏴 Braw in US Senator Rand Paul press release | Atlantic Council
item.description: On May 18, Transatlantic Security Initiative senior fellow Elisabeth Braw was mentioned in a press release by US Senator Rand Paul discussing the REPO Implementation Act of 2025. The post Braw in US Senator Rand Paul press release appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🕵️ Braw in Prospect on what constitutes an act of war | Atlantic Council
item.description: On May 18, Transatlantic Security Initiative senior fellow Elisabeth Braw was published in Prospect in an article about what constitutes an act of war, and the ongoing trial of the owner of the Nord Stream pipeline and its insurers. The post Braw in Prospect on what constitutes…

item.title: 🌏 Strike missiles and cheap drones: how Southeast Asia can deter China | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Southeast Asian countries looking for security against China should shift the weight of their defence spending to deploying highly mobile, mostly inexpensive equipment that it would struggle to counter. These would be strike missiles, cheap ...

item.title: 🌍 G-7 Finance Ministers Discuss Economic Fallout of Iran War | Foreign Policy
item.description: Rising oil prices and growing bond market volatility have raised fears of a global recession.

item.title: 🌏 Farmers Endure Militarization in the Philippines’ ‘Massacre Capital’ | The Diplomat
item.description: Soldiers claim 19 rebel deaths in “legitimate encounter” last month, but questions linger over the killings in Negros, which included a student leader, a journalist, a local farmer, and others.

item.title: 🌍 Iran Is Winning the Vibe War | Foreign Policy
item.description: Disinformation experts need a new framework in the era of AI slop.

item.title: 🏛️ ‘Going to change everything’: Special Forces joins Army’s next-gen C2 prototype experiments | Breaking Defense
item.description: After hearing how the 4th Infantry Division was using its new C2 tech, 10th Special Forces Group got in on the action, including at the recent Ivy Mass exercise.

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine Has a New War Strategy—and It’s Working | Foreign Policy
item.description: A year ago, the Ukrainian government decided to take the fight directly to Russia. It hasn’t looked back since.
